ops.body:
## `--fresh-epoch`: one-shot clean-slate promotion (commits e246a077, c7bb75b6)

### Why it exists
When the frozen promotion eval is wiped and refrozen clean-slate, the first
clean-data retrain must NOT be gated against incumbents whose training data AND
stored eval probs came from the corrupted era. `--fresh-epoch` gates the
candidate "as if no incumbent existed." It is a deliberate one-shot cutover
flag, not a reusable skip-the-gate switch.

### Watcher lane (`retrain_watcher`, Option A)
- Skips `_load_incumbent_probs` + the incumbent-relative `corrected_gate` /
  `watcher_promotable` entirely.
- Keeps every already-absolute check: spend cap and mlx parity.
- One absolute refusal: candidate sentinel AUC must be **finite AND > 0.5**
  (`not (np.isfinite(eval_auc) and eval_auc > 0.5)` → raise). NaN counts as a
  refusal — sklearn returns NaN AUC on a single-class frame and `nan <= 0.5` is
  False, so a bare `<= 0.5` check would let it through (fixed in c7bb75b6, M2).
  This is a degenerate-state crash, not a quality gate; 0.5 is the no-skill
  anchor, and there is deliberately no floor above it.
- One-shot guard `FreshEpochError` (`_refuse_scored_frame`): **scans
  `<eval_root>/probs/*.json` stored digests directly** (NOT registry versions —
  c7bb75b6, L1) so an orphan probs file from a pruned version still trips the
  cutover. Refuses if any file's `meta.dataset_digest` == the current frame
  digest. Names the offending file(s).
- Runs BEFORE any Tinker spend: both the one-shot guard (fresh) and the
  incumbent-gate load (normal lane — `_load_incumbent_probs` + threshold, via
  the `IncumbentGate` NamedTuple) are validated before `tk.train_lora`, so a
  missing/corrupt probs file fails free (c7bb75b6, M1 — a regression fix; the
  fresh-epoch refactor had moved the load after training).
- Registration, prune, `write_probs`, kickstart unchanged. The serving model
  stays live until the candidate registers over it. `should_retrain`
  unchanged. Journal line: `fresh-epoch promoted <ver> (candidate AUC X, no
  incumbent gate); ...`.

### Gate lane (`retrain_gate`)
- Routes through `gate_promotable`'s no-incumbent path (`incumbent_metrics=None`
  when `incumbent is None or fresh_epoch`). Journal-marked. No probs store on
  this lane, so no reuse guard.

### cc-notes journal mirror (C1, hardened in c7bb75b6 M3/M4)
- `promotion.journal()` mirrors each line to the "cc-steer retrain journal"
  cc-notes log via `Journal(Path.cwd(), ...)`, best-effort. `journal.py`'s
  parse is hardened so no cc-notes output shape (null, `[null]`, a bare dict,
  an entry without `id`) can make the mirror raise after promotion side effects.
- The weekly retrain launchd agent now sets `WorkingDirectory` to the journal
  repo (`render_retrain`/`install_retrain` take `journal_repo`), so the
  unattended agent's cwd is a repo and the mirror fires. Without `--journal-repo`
  at install time, no WorkingDirectory is set and the mirror no-ops (opt-in,
  like the pipeline agent). NOTE: the plist must be reinstalled after a release
  for this to take effect.

### CLI wiring
`--fresh-epoch` is a flag on `cc-steer retrain`, threaded keyword-only into
`retrain_gate`/`retrain_watcher`, and rejected alongside the watcher bootstrap
paths (`--seed-incumbent-probs`, `--register-adapter`).
